title: Phaius terrestris
intro: Phaius terrestris, also known as the terrestrial phaius, is an orchid species native to tropical Asia that is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Phaius terrestris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditionally, Phaius terrestris is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tonics, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulas for its purported health benefits.
taxonomy: Phaius terrestris is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. Phaius terrestris is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae, one of the two main subfamilies of the Orchidaceae. It is also classified within the tribe Arethuseae, a group of orchids known for their distinctive flowers and growth habits. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Phaius terrestris and other related species.
